regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces escalating multi-front pressure from US military blockade and carrier deployments while experiencing acute internal divisions between negotiation-seeking civilians and hardline IRGC commanders, compounded by economic strain from bread shortages and soaring food prices despite paradoxical claims of increased oil revenues.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On April 26, 2026, President Trump extended an indefinite ceasefire with Iran to allow unified proposal development, dispatching envoys Witkoff and Kushner to Islamabad while Iran's IRGC Navy seized vessels and mapped undersea cables in response to Trump's 24-hour Strait of Hormuz ultimatum; simultaneously, Iran's negotiating team head Ghalibaf quit amid internal disagreements blocking diplomatic progress.
